title: Hezbollah sees war in Lebanon as a chance for political resurgence
subhed: Emboldened by renewed support from Iran, the Shiite movement is aiming to influence the conditions under which the crisis is resolved and reclaim its previous central role in Lebanese politics.

Ebola outbreak: Anger mounts in Kenya over quarantine center for American patients
subhed: About 200 kilometers from the capital, the town of Nanyuki witnessed another day of protests on Tuesday against the proposed American quarantine center, set to be established on an air base. The day was marked by clashes between demonstrators and law enforcement.

title: How France uncovered a network of propaganda sites linked to Beijing
subhed: Thirteen fake news websites launched in 2025 promoted China in a one-sided manner. An investigation by the French government agency Viginum has established that they were directly connected to CGTN, China's state-run television channel.

title: 'Disclosure Day': Steven Spielberg goes back to the future
subhed: After two more personal, retrospective feature films, the American filmmaker revisits his passion for extraterrestrials.

title: 'Operation Pushkin': Rare books, Georgian con artists and the shadow of the Kremlin
subhed: For two years, Georgian criminals orchestrated the theft of rare editions of works by Alexander Pushkin from major European libraries. The trial of five of them begins in Paris on June 9. It could reveal whether Russia was involved.

title: 'I am a libertarian and belong to no party': The octogenarian, his anarchist friends and the mystery of a bomb at McDonald's
subhed: In July 2025, an explosive device and a torn Israeli flag were left in front of a McDonald's in France. After a 10-month investigation, an 85-year-old former press photographer, who claims to be outraged by the war in Gaza, emerged as the main suspect, while a possible accomplice died by suicide on May 7, just before an anti-terrorism operation.

title: Pope Leo XIV quotes Gandalf, highlighting Tolkien's close ties with Catholicism
subhed: A Lord of the Rings reference in the last papal encyclical has sparked a great deal of commentary. While it can be read as a response to the way Silicon Valley libertarians have appropriated Tolkien's writing, it also highlights the English author's religious convictions.
